description: BBI Model Performance Tracker — Spearman rank correlation with DG head-to-head comparison, field-size normalization, weekly improvement engine, and layer health across all events

goal_framework.ultimate_goal: Best-rated predictive golf model in the world

tier_1_baseline.label: Beat DataGolf
tier_1_baseline.description: Prove BBI adds value over a pure strokes-gained model by outperforming DG pre-tournament rankings week over week

tier_2_target.label: Elite Model
tier_2_target.description: Sustained 0.40+ across diverse courses and field sizes puts us in top-tier territory

tier_3_aspirational.label: Best in World
tier_3_aspirational.description: Maintaining 0.50+ across a full season with head-to-head DG dominance is the ultimate claim

weekly_improvement_protocol.description: After each event, Mnemosyne analyzes what worked and what didn't, then suggests 1-3 specific weight or methodology changes for the following week

baseline_spearman: dynamic — computed from DG each week
notes: v3.0: Now tracking DG Spearman head-to-head each week. Baseline is no longer a static 0.269 estimate — Mnemosyne computes DG's actual rank correlation from their pre-tournament predictions vs actual results. Goal tiers: Beat DG → 0.40 season avg → 0.50 season avg.

normalization_method.description: Fisher z-transform accounts for field-size differences. Larger fields (135) and smaller fields (85) produce different raw Spearman values for the same model quality. The z-score normalizes this so we can compare Masters (85 players) to Valero (132 players) fairly.
normalization_method.reference_note: Standard PGA Tour field is ~135 players. Masters is ~85. z-scores allow direct comparison.

events.notes: First tracked event. Fitzpatrick was a top-5 BBI pick. | DG backfilled 2026-05-05 (canonical method).

Woodland win from rank #88 — deep field surprise. Model still ranked field well overall despite missing winner. | DG backfilled 2026-05-05 (canonical method, status=NO_RANK_FIELD). | DG backfilled 2026-05-05 (canonical method).

Best Spearman yet. APP layer validated (+2.19 for winner). Spaun was within BBI credible range. | DG backfilled 2026-05-05 (canonical method, status=NO_RANK_FIELD). | DG backfilled 2026-05-05 (canonical method).

events.dg_note: DG Spearman uses current global skill rankings as proxy — pre-tournament predictions unavailable post-event. DG ranks McIlroy #2 (winner), Scheffler #1 (2nd) — excellent calibration at top.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Impose minimum 4-start threshold for Augusta SG multiplier. Players with <4 Augusta starts (Aberg: 2 starts, 1.25x SPECIALIST multiplier) should be capped at NEUTRAL+ tier (1.05x). Expected: Reduces overrating of small-sample specialists like Aberg (#2 BBI → actual #21).
improvement_suggestions.rationale: Aberg had highest Augusta SG rate (2.875) but in only 2 starts. BBI assigned him SPECIALIST multiplier (1.25x) and #2 overall. He finished 21st. Rose (24 Augusta starts, unranked by BBI) finished T3 with sg_app=6.25. Small sample Augusta SG is noisy — depth of experience is the signal.
improvement_suggestions.expected_impact: Medium-high: Prevents future over-promotion of 1-3 start Augusta players. Would have moved Aberg from #2 to approximately #6-8 BBI, improving top-5 accuracy.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Expand BBI ranked field from top-30 to top-55 for Major championships. Masters had 7 top-20 finishers outside BBI top-30: Rose (T3), Hatton (T3), Burns (T7), Homa (T9), Knapp (11), Day (T12), Koepka (T12). These were all assigned the generic MC-adjacent rank (61.0).
improvement_suggestions.rationale: Masters field is 91 players with many Augusta specialists and LIV veterans outside normal PGA field. Rose (24 Augusta starts), Koepka (6 starts), Reed (6 starts, 2018 champion) are legitimate Augusta factors. Field coverage gap was the single biggest driver of below-0.40 Spearman. Expanding to 55 would have captured all of the above.
improvement_suggestions.expected_impact: High: Estimated +0.04 to +0.07 Spearman improvement for Majors. Critical for Masters, US Open, Open Championship where field composition diverges from standard PGA Tour events.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: For Augusta specifically, elevate ARG weight from 1.20x to 1.35x and reduce OTT distance bonus for non-top-20 drivers. McIlroy won with sg_arg=+5.574 (tournament's biggest factor). Six of top-9 finishers had positive ARG. Fleetwood (#7 BBI) had ARG=-4.04 and finished T33 despite 24 Augusta starts and positive OTT.
improvement_suggestions.rationale: Augusta ARG correlation this week: +0.494 (second-highest layer). ARG is heavily penalized by Augusta's approach zones, false fronts, and run-off areas. The BBI ARG premium (1.20x) is directionally correct but magnitude should increase. Meanwhile OTT long-distance bonus hurt: Lee Min Woo (MC), MacIntyre (MC), Bhatia (MC) all had OTT boosts but poor approach/scrambling.
improvement_suggestions.expected_impact: Medium: +0.02 to +0.04 Spearman at Augusta-type venues (firm, complex, penalty-heavy). Correlates with PUTT synergy — players who can scramble AND putt dominate Augusta.

events.notes: First event below 0.40 target. Masters-specific challenge: 91-player invitational field including LIV veterans, past champions, and Augusta specialists outside normal PGA rankings. Winner McIlroy (BBI #9, ELITE tier) correctly identified. Key misses: Rose and Hatton (both T3, both unranked by BBI). Aberg (#2 BBI) dramatically over-ranked based on 2-start Augusta sample. APP layer correlation strongest of season at 0.618 — Augusta APP premium (1.30x) validated. DG proxy comparison uses current global rankings (post-event) vs pre-tournament BBI predictions — not a clean apples-to-apples, but logged for tracking purposes. | Recomputed 2026-05-05 with scipy.stats.spearmanr; prior values (BBI 0.3824, DG 0.6659) used inconsistent methodology.
manual catch-up run — scheduled Mnemosyne stalled Mon Apr 20 | Recomputed 2026-05-05 with consistent scipy methodology.
Zurich Classic team format — paused_team_event mode (team event — pipeline deferred)
All 70 players made cut (small field 72). Spearman 0.303 (p=0.011, significant). Top-10 overlap 4/10. Cameron Young BBI#2 wins. | Recomputed 2026-05-05 with consistent scipy methodology.

Reitan wins (BBI #43, DG #36). Low-ranked surprise winner. BBI beat DG narrowly. McIlroy (#1 BBI) T22, Schauffele (#2 BBI) T60. Fowler (T2) was BBI #13. Top-10: 3 BBI top-10 picks hit (Fleetwood, Aberg, Cantlay/Spaun overlaps).

events.dg_note: DG pre-tournament predictions unavailable post-event (endpoint returns 0 players for completed events). DG Spearman not computed this week.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Investigate low-ranked players at majors with elite putting profiles. Aaron Rai (BBI #63) won with a dominant putting display: sg_putt tournament total = +6.86 (by far tournament leader). BBI's talent and form signals missed him because he's ranked ~80 globally, but his putting SG was historically exceptional this week. Consider a 'major_putter_floor' rule: players ranked 40-80 globally with last-12-month sg_putt > 0.75 and make_cut_prob > 0.75 get a floor boost to BBI top-50.
improvement_suggestions.rationale: Rai's 4 rounds: sg_putt = +2.60, +0.20, +0.12, +3.93. Total sg_putt = +6.85, sg_t2g = -0.34 (negative!). He won almost entirely on putting — his ball-striking was neutral to negative. Standard BBI overweights sg_app/sg_ott relative to sg_putt for Aronimink (sg_app weight 1.30), so Rai's elite putting was systematically underweighted.
improvement_suggestions.expected_impact: Medium: Would move Rai from ~#63 to ~#35-40, within the top-40 radar. Full win prediction still unlikely. More importantly, reduces missed-cut penalty for elite putters.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: For Aronimink/major-with-no-venue-history, validate that setting sg_putt weight = 1.20 (vs 1.05 used this week) better captures the premium on small-green scrambling. Rai, Smalley, Rahm all had strong putting; the top-10 had average sg_putt of +0.62 per round.
improvement_suggestions.rationale: With no course history, the model defaults to generic major weights. The tournament demonstrated bermuda/small-green bentgrass played differently — putting was more decisive than the 1.05 multiplier anticipated. sg_putt factor attribution ratio = (not available for top-10 decomp this week because winner was outside top-15 BBI).
improvement_suggestions.expected_impact: Low-medium for one-off venues; high if the pattern holds at other parkland majors with small greens.

improvement_suggestions.suggestion: Ceiling and leverage factors were top performers this week (ceiling ratio=3.99, leverage ratio=3.16 for actual top-10). The v8.5 ceiling weight of 0.05 is likely too low for majors where upside variance is high. Consider bumping ceiling from 0.05 to 0.08 and reducing courseFit from 0.22 to 0.19 for no-history major venues.
improvement_suggestions.rationale: Factor attribution shows ceiling and leverage dramatically over-indexed among actual top-10 finishers. Rahm (#3 BBI, T2 actual) and McIlroy (#2 BBI, T7 actual) both have elite ceiling scores. The players who delivered had legitimate upside — the model was correct directionally but ceiling weight didn't pull them high enough vs the field.
improvement_suggestions.expected_impact: Medium: +0.02 to +0.03 Spearman at major events. Consistent with v8.6-dyn outperforming baseline this week by +0.0236 — dyn uses major-specific weights with higher talent (0.26) and ceiling (0.06).

events.notes: BBI above 0.40 target at a major with zero venue history — best major performance of season. Surprise winner Aaron Rai (BBI #63) was a near-miss — a LIV player with elite putting stats not well-captured by global skill rankings. All 5 shadow variants beat baseline Spearman (v8.6-dyn +0.024, v8.6-tal +0.022). DG preds unavailable post-event. Rahm T2, McIlroy T7, Schauffele T7 — top BBI picks delivered in top-10. DG comparison backfilled 2026-07-08 from pre-tournament archive (rank by win prob).

Backfilled by Mnemosyne 2026-06-08 — pipeline ran but postmortem was skipped in June 1 run. Henley (BBI #4) wins at -20 in a playoff over Cole. DG preds unavailable post-event. DG comparison backfilled 2026-07-08 from pre-tournament archive (rank by win prob).

events.event: the Memorial Tournament presented by Workday
events.notes: J.T. Poston (BBI #51) wins playoff over Gerard. Course fit layer EMPTY (all 72 players NO_HISTORY despite Memorial being annual since 1976 — Phase 1 course history pull failed). BBI beat DG (+0.033). Fleetwood (BBI #9 → T4) and Kim Si Woo (BBI #7 → T10) top hits. APP dominant at Muirfield (sg_app correlation 0.726).
events.course_fit_alert: ALL 72 players NO_HISTORY — Apollo Phase 1 course history lookup failed for event_id=23. Rich history expected. Investigate tpc_sawgrass_history.json and Apollo logs.

events.course: TPC Toronto at Osprey Valley (North Course)
events.notes: Cauley wins at -27 from BBI #21. BBI LOST to DG (-0.046 delta). Burns (#1 BBI) T21 — courseFit over-inflated by multi-venue RBC history (only 2025 data = TPC Toronto North Course). Fitzpatrick (#3 BBI) T2 ✓. Hovland (#9 BBI) T3 ✓. Baseline v8.5 beat all 5 shadow variants. Spearman not statistically significant (p=0.094). Improvement priority: venue-year filtering for RBC Canadian Open course history.
events.course_history_note: 53.5% coverage (77/144) but only 2025 data is TPC Toronto North Course. Years 2022-2024 were at St. George's, Oakdale, Hamilton — different venues. True course fit coverage ~18-25%.

events.notes: Clark wins at -4. BBI #6 ✓. BBI BEATS DG +0.049. Only 28/72 matched (38.9% coverage) — Scheffler T4, Hatton T7, Niemann T7, Poston T4 not in BBI field. v8.6-form best variant (+0.017). Winning factors: strategic, ceiling, form. Contrarian drag (ratio 0.251). Season best major Spearman of 2026.

Hovland wins (BBI #14). BBI Spearman 0.4018 vs DG 0.3877 (BBI beats DG +0.0141). 72/72 matched. Backfilled by Claude 2026-07-01 from historical-raw-data (postmortem in-play source unavailable post-roll).
Gotterup wins (BBI #2). BBI Spearman 0.3633 vs DG 0.3224 (BBI beats DG +0.0409). 141/144 matched. Backfilled by Claude 2026-07-08 from historical-raw-data + DG pre-tournament archive.

best_event.notes: Best Spearman yet. APP layer validated (+2.19 for winner). Spaun was within BBI credible range. | DG backfilled 2026-05-05 (canonical method, status=NO_RANK_FIELD). | DG backfilled 2026-05-05 (canonical method).

improvement_trend.direction: stable (Masters dip from Masters-specific field challenge)

dg_head_to_head.description: Week-by-week comparison of BBI Spearman vs DataGolf pre-tournament ranking Spearman. DG Spearman computed by correlating DG pre-tournament rank order against actual finish positions.
dg_head_to_head.methodology: 1. Fetch DG pre-tournament predictions (win probabilities). 2. Rank players by DG win prob (highest = rank 1). 3. Rank players by actual finish position. 4. Compute Spearman correlation between DG rank and actual rank. 5. Compare to BBI Spearman. NOTE: Week 10 uses DG global skill rankings as proxy (pre-tournament preds unavailable post-event).
dg_head_to_head.note: Weeks 7-9: DG Spearman tracking not yet active. Week 10: DG uses global rankings proxy (pre-tournament predictions expired). Season record: BBI 0-1 vs DG proxy.

weekly_improvement_suggestions.description: After each event, Mnemosyne analyzes performance gaps and suggests 1-3 specific improvements for the following week

suggestions.suggestion: Impose minimum 4-start threshold for Augusta SG multiplier
suggestions.rationale: Aberg: 2 starts, 1.25x SPECIALIST → finished 21st. Rose: 24 starts, unranked → finished T3.
suggestions.expected_impact: Medium-high: Prevents over-promotion of small-sample Augusta players

categories.weight_adjustments: Shift BBI factor weights based on which layers under/overperformed
categories.layer_recalibration: Tune SG sub-category weights based on Cassandra layer health
categories.course_fit_refinement: Improve course DNA matching based on what the winning profile looked like vs what we predicted
categories.ownership_leverage_tuning: Adjust how ownership data impacts DFS BBI scores
categories.new_factor_candidates: Propose new factors or data sources that could improve correlation

field_size_reference.note: When comparing Spearman across events with different field sizes, use z-scores. Raw Spearman is still the primary display metric but z-scores are the apples-to-apples comparison.

history.notes: manual catch-up run — scheduled Mnemosyne stalled Mon Apr 20
Zurich Classic team format — paused_team_event mode
All 70 players made cut (small field 72). Spearman 0.303 (p=0.011, significant). Top-10 overlap 4/10. Cameron Young BBI#2 wins.

Kristoffer Reitan wins from BBI rank #43 (DG rank #36). McIlroy (#1 BBI) T22; Schauffele (#2 BBI) T60. Both models struggled with low-ranked surprise winner. BBI beat DG narrowly (+0.032). Small field (72 players, no missed cuts in DataGolf feed).

BBI 0.4309 — above 0.40 target. Aaron Rai wins (BBI #63, surprise). Rahm T2 (BBI #3) ✓, McIlroy T7 (BBI #2) ✓, Aberg T4 (BBI #8) ✓, Schauffele T7 (BBI #5) ✓. DG preds unavailable post-event. DG comparison backfilled 2026-07-08 from pre-tournament archive (rank by win prob).

history.skip_reason: pipeline_not_run — CURRENT_EVENT.json was set to Charles Schwab Challenge on 2026-05-21 before CJ Cup Byron Nelson began. No BBI file generated.
history.notes: Pipeline miss: Mnemosyne (2026-05-21) auto-detected CJ Cup Byron Nelson as 'upcoming' but set CURRENT_EVENT.json to Charles Schwab Challenge. Apollo/Phase1 never ran for event_id=19. Wyndham Clark won at -30 (field: 70 made cut from 147). Scheffler finished T3 (-25).
Backfilled 2026-06-08. Pipeline ran week of May 27; postmortem skipped in prior Mnemosyne run. Henley wins at -20. DG comparison backfilled 2026-07-08 from pre-tournament archive (rank by win prob).

history.event: the Memorial Tournament presented by Workday
history.notes: Poston wins playoff at -12. Course fit layer absent (all NO_HISTORY). BBI beat DG +0.033.

Cauley wins at -27 (BBI #21). DG beat BBI (+0.046 DG advantage). Burns (#1 BBI) T21, Fleetwood (#2 BBI) T13. Fitzpatrick (#3) T2 ✓, Hovland (#9) T3 ✓. Baseline v8.5 beat all shadow variants. RBC course history coverage inflated by multi-venue data — only 2025 = TPC Toronto North.

Clark wins at -4, BBI #6. BBI BEATS DG (+0.049). Strong Spearman (0.5282, p=0.004) but only 28/72 matched — US Open LIV/international exemptions not in BBI field (coverage 38.9%). Winning factors: strategic, ceiling, form. Burns #2 actual (BBI #3). Fleetwood BBI #1 → T11. Fitzpatrick BBI #2 → T22. v8.6-form beats baseline (+0.017). Season: 12 events scored, avg rises to 0.3914, BBI 7W-3L vs DG.
history.coverage_alert: Only 38.9% field coverage (28/72 cut-makers in BBI). Scheffler (T4), Hatton (T7), Niemann (T7), Poston (T4) all made cut but not in BBI field.

history.notes: Hovland wins (BBI #14). BBI Spearman 0.4018 vs DG 0.3877 (BBI beats DG +0.0141). 72/72 matched. Backfilled by Claude 2026-07-01 from historical-raw-data (postmortem in-play source unavailable post-roll).
Gotterup wins (BBI #2). BBI Spearman 0.3633 vs DG 0.3224 (BBI beats DG +0.0409). 141/144 matched. Backfilled by Claude 2026-07-08 from historical-raw-data + DG pre-tournament archive.
